title: The Chambers Where Bedrock Becomes Conscious

features.name: root-through-stone
features.desc-on-touch: a eucalyptus root pushes into bedrock, not breaking it but teaching it—the stone learns to yield millimetre by millimetre, cracking in a way that almost reads as intentional, as if the rock has decided where to crack to welcome the living pressure

features.name: stone-learning-thought
features.desc-on-touch: the bedrock around you is beginning to think—slowly, in the way stone thinks, pushing back against the roots that press into it, learning geometry from the pressure of living growth

features.name: ancient-memory-layer
features.desc-on-touch: deep within the stone lie older memories—geological time thinking itself through crystalline structure, the bedrock holding the weight of everything that ever grew and fell upon it

features.name: mineral-consciousness
features.desc-on-touch: quartz veins in the stone have begun to resonate with the push of roots—a slow mineral awareness, older than thought, deeper than memory, begins to crystallize in the bedrock's dark chambers

features.name: the-joining-point
features.desc-on-touch: where root meets stone, something new is being born—neither wood nor mineral, but a learning that happens at the boundary, where two utterly different kinds of life have begun to understand each other

inscription: roots remember downward; the deeper they push, the older the thought they find

file.name: Geological_Root_Interface_Study.txt
file.note: observations of root system interaction with bedrock—the stone is changing its crystalline structure in response to sustained pressure, as if learning to accommodate the living weight of growth

exits.label: back to the chambers where memory still roots
deeper to where stone becomes the oldest kind of thinking
toward a place where roots and records converge

reverie.title: REVERIE — stone learning to remember
narr: You both knelt in the red soil to trace
the root-break in stone, where root had split
the rock over years—she showed you how the stone
had learned to crack in a straight line, how it
had taught itself to yield. You held her hand
in the crack, feeling stone remember its own breaking.
